steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence is: "Ann Quinby of [blank] an important role in the campaign..." The blank must connect "Ann Quinby of" to "an important role" by completing the subject with a location and supplying a main verb. The sentence needs one complete independent clause — subject plus verb with no interrupting punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Kentucky. Played’: A period after "Kentucky" creates a fragment ("Played an important role..." has no subject). ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Kentucky: played’: A colon after "Kentucky" cuts the subject "Ann Quinby of Kentucky" off from its verb, making both halves non-sentences. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Kentucky; played’: A semicolon must connect two independent clauses; "Ann Quinby of Kentucky" is not a clause, so this is a misuse. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Kentucky played’: No punctuation between subject and verb. "Ann Quinby of Kentucky played an important role..." is one complete, correct sentence. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
